perturbation.original_text: 1.21. "MANUFACTURING FACILITY" means the manufacturing facility to be constructed by Parent on behalf of the Manufacturing Company at the Site in accordance with the provisions of Article 3.6 which will be capable of producing 14,000 kg per annum of pure Astaxanthin.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21. "MANUFACTURING FACILITY" means the manufacturing facility to be constructed by Parent on behalf of the Manufacturing Company at the Site in accordance with the provisions of Article 3.6.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "which will be capable of producing 14,000 kg per annum of pure Astaxanthin" from the definition of 'Manufacturing Facility' in Article 1.21, we introduce ambiguity about the facility's production capacity. This directly contradicts Article 14.4 where T&L warrants to Igene that "the Manufacturing Facility will be a turnkey project capable of producing 14,000 kg per year of pure Astaxanthin."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.4. T&L represents and warrants to Igene that (i) the Manufacturing Facility will be constructed in accordance with the terms of the Construction Agreement, free and clear of all liens, claims and encumbrances and (ii) the Manufacturing Facility will be a turnkey project capable of producing 14,000 kg per year of pure Astaxanthin.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'which will be capable of producing 14,000 kg per annum of pure Astaxanthin' from the definition of 'Manufacturing Facility' creates ambiguity about the facility's production capacity. This directly contradicts Article 14.4, which warrants that 'the Manufacturing Facility will be a turnkey project capable of producing 14,000 kg per year of pure Astaxanthin.' The omission in Article 1.21 weakens the guarantee in Article 14.4, potentially leading to disputes about whether the facility is required to produce that specific amount.

perturbation.original_text: 8.4. In the event either T&L or Igene wishes to market Astaxanthin for uses other than in the Field of Agreement, including but not limited to the use of Astaxanthin as a neutraceutical or otherwise for direct human consumption, either T&L or Igene, as the case may be, shall be permitted to purchase reasonable quantities of Astaxanthin from the Operating Company for use in the manufacture of such products at the Average Market Price. The Joint Venture will manufacture such Astaxanthin to the standard required for its business, and each Party desiring to market Astaxanthin for other uses shall process the product acquired from the Operating Company at its own cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.4. In the event either T&L or Igene wishes to market Astaxanthin for uses other than in the Field of Agreement, including but not limited to the use of Astaxanthin as a neutraceutical or otherwise for direct human consumption, either T&L or Igene, as the case may be, shall be permitted to purchase reasonable quantities of Astaxanthin from the Operating Company for use in the manufacture of such products. The Joint Venture will manufacture such Astaxanthin to the standard required for its business, and each Party desiring to market Astaxanthin for other uses shall process the product acquired from the Operating Company at its own cost and expense.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'at the Average Market Price' we eliminate the pricing for Astaxanthin purchases outside the Field of Agreement. This creates an ambiguity, because the sentence 'either T&L or Igene, as the case may be, shall be permitted to purchase reasonable quantities of Astaxanthin from the Operating Company for use in the manufacture of such products' doesn't specify the price anymore. Without this pricing clause, Article 8.4 becomes unenforceable, as the terms for the Astaxanthin purchase are incomplete creating an in-text contradiction of its application
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.4. In the event either T&L or Igene wishes to market Astaxanthin for uses other than in the Field of Agreement, including but not limited to the use of Astaxanthin as a neutraceutical or otherwise for direct human consumption, either T&L or Igene, as the case may be, shall be permitted to purchase reasonable quantities of Astaxanthin from the Operating Company for use in the manufacture of such products. The Joint Venture will manufacture such Astaxanthin to the standard required for its business, and each Party desiring to market Astaxanthin for other uses shall process the product acquired from the Operating Company at its own cost and expense.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'at the Average Market Price' from Article 8.4 creates an omission in pricing, making the clause regarding the purchase of Astaxanthin for uses outside the Field of Agreement unenforceable. This contradicts the initial intent of Article 8.4, which aimed to establish clear terms for such purchases, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. It fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.3. In the event of a contradiction between the terms of this Article 11 and any Support Services Agreement, the terms of the Support Services Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.3. In the event of a contradiction between the terms of this Article 11 and any Support Services Agreement, the terms of this Article 11 shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: By inverting the clause 'the terms of the Support Services Agreement shall govern' and changing it to 'the terms of this Article 11 shall govern', it introduces a contradiction in the contract. Article 11 now claims primacy over the Support Services Agreements, but the Support Services Agreements are referenced in Article 11.1 as being 'substantially in the form attached hereto as Appendix 11.1.1 and Appendix 11.1.2'. If there's a contradiction, the modified Article 11.3 invalidates the purpose of having the Support Services Agreements in the first place, due to saying the terms of Article 11 are the one's that must be followed, causing an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1. Contemporaneously with the execution of this Agreement, each Party will enter into an agreement or agreements substantially in the form attached hereto as Appendix 11.1.1 and Appendix 11.1.2 pursuant to which each Party or its Affiliates shall provide the services identified for it below (the "Support Services Agreements", and each a "Support Services Agreement"). The Support Services Agreements shall provide that the parties will provide the following services to the Joint Venture:
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 11.3 directly contradicts the purpose and enforcement of the Support Services Agreements referenced in 11.1. By stating that Article 11 governs in case of conflict, the changed text undermines the legally binding agreements (Appendix 11.1.1 and 11.1.2) which the original text prioritized. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as Article 11 now claims precedence over agreements it relies on, causing uncertainty and potential unenforceability of the Support Services Agreements as mentioned in Article 11.1. This fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction as essential language (the supremacy of the Support Services Agreement) is altered, creating a direct conflict.
